name: kalibrate-rtl
note: GSM frequency scanner: sweeps a band (GSM850/900/1800/1900) for active base-station carriers by locking onto their FCCH/SCH bursts, reporting each ARFCN, its power and the radio's clock-frequency offset (ppm). The kalibrate-rtl fork drives an RTL-SDR; sibling forks exist for HackRF and UHD. The first step before any capture: it tells you which ARFCNs have a live BTS.

name: gr-gsm
note: GNU Radio blocks and tools to receive and demodulate the GSM downlink from an SDR. grgsm_livemon tunes a found ARFCN, demodulates the GMSK bursts, decodes the control channels (BCCH/CCCH/SDCCH) and forwards the frames as GSMTAP over UDP to Wireshark. The de-facto open-source GSM receiver, successor to airprobe.

name: IMSI-catcher
note: Python tool that reads the GSMTAP stream produced by gr-gsm and pulls the IMSI / TMSI identities the network pages over the air, printing the cellphones it sees around you. Passive identity exposure demonstrator and a building block for IMSI-catcher (fake-BTS) detection.

name: Kraken (A5/1 cracker)
note: GPU/CPU cracker that recovers an A5/1 session key from a captured keystream segment using the ~1.6–2 TB A5/1 rainbow tables (the Berlin A5/1 Security Project). Recovers Kc, which decrypts the rest of a captured call/SMS session. Old but the reference open A5/1 attack; requires the bulky precomputed tables and a known-keystream slice from the capture.
